title: hypodaphnis zenkeri
intro: Hypodaphnis zenkeri, also known as the Queen of the Forest, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical forests of West Africa. It is a climber that produces fragrant flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a treatment for wounds and infections. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are applied topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
